title: Content Strategy vs. Content Creation Quiz

questions.question: Why do many founders see high traffic but zero leads from their content?

options.text: They are focusing on 'Content that Impresses' peers rather than 'Content that Converts' customers
Google is hiding their content from buyers

questions.explanation: Traffic from peers (other experts) rarely turns into revenue. Strategic content targets the person 'one step behind' who actually has the problem you solve.

questions.question: What is the 'Job' of Top of Funnel (TOFU) content?

options.text: To compare your product to a competitor
To attract a specific audience through educational value
To provide a technical documentation for current users

questions.explanation: TOFU content is about discovery. It addresses broad pain points and educational needs to attract your ICP into your ecosystem.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is the ideal ratio for creation vs. distribution?
questions.explanation: Most founders over-invest in writing and under-invest in ensureing people actually see it. The 20/80 ratio ensures your hard work reaches its target.

questions.question: What 'Technical Founder Advantage' is highlighted as a key to content success?

options.text: The ability to write complex code

questions.explanation: Technical founders are naturally good at documentation and building systems. Content marketing, when viewed as a repeatable system, plays directly to these strengths.

questions.question: Which company is cited as a case study for successful content-led growth with $0 ad spend?
questions.explanation: Plausible used a 'boring' but consistent content strategy focusing on the pain points of their ICP (privacy) to grow to $3.1M ARR without ads.

questions.question: Audit one of your recent blog posts or content ideas. Who is the target audience (be specific)? What is the 'one step behind' problem they are facing? What is the one specific next step you want them to take after reading?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate if the founder identifies a specific ICP (not just 'everyone') and if the 'next step' is a logical conversion point (e.g., newsletter signup from TOFU). Push back if the audience is too broad.
